Description: Intersections modified by making flyovers, underpasses, flyover loops at normal intersections. e.g. Punjabi Bagh chowk, Dhaula Kuan, Mukarba chowk
Junction points of more than 2 roads or a stretch of single major road having more than 2 minor roads joining within 500 meter stretch

Places where there is facility of changing of different modes of transport like city buses, TSRs, Gramin sewa, RTVs, interstate buses, E-rickshaw at the same place. e.g. ISBT, Peera garhi chowk, Mukarba chowk

Classification does not completely segregate one category from the other - some metro stations are also exchange hubs (e.g. Peera garhi chowk), some Exchange hubs are also multilevel intersections (e.g. Mukarba chowk)

Intersections of different types are more prone to accidents. Multilevel intersections are the most dangerous. Other accident-prone places are places of high footfall (bus stands, metro stations, exchange hubs) indicating lack of proper systematic and planned last mile connectivity
